message:
refactor: complete typestate migration in the critical path (br-jkcka.8)

Closes the jkcka epic's workspace migration: deprecates the
ambiguous CryptographicallyVerified surface and migrates the single
production call site (fcp-host live request path) to the typed
verify_unbound → claims_unbound path.

Changes:
- fcp-core/src/capability.rs:
  * #[deprecated] on CryptographicallyVerified marker
  * #[deprecated] on CapabilityVerifier::verify
  * #[deprecated] on VerifiedToken type alias
  * Targeted #[allow(deprecated)] on in-crate internals that still
    legitimately implement the legacy trait (Sealed, AnyVerified,
    Serialize, the verify() method itself, impl blocks). External
    consumers see the deprecation; fcp-core's own build stays clean.
- fcp-core/src/connector.rs:
  * `Polling` trait: start_polling / stop_polling / poll_now take
    &CapabilityToken<BoundVerified> (full 5/5 verification)
  * `Webhook` trait: register_handler takes BoundVerified
  * These archetype traits have no production implementors yet, so
    the signature change is cost-free right now and costs-of-entry
    for future impls.
- fcp-core/src/policy.rs:
  * Simulation docstring points at verify_bound / verify_unbound
    instead of the ambiguous verify()
- fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:
  * The gateway live-request path (the ORIGINAL motivating example
    of the jkcka epic) now calls verify_unbound() explicitly,
    producing CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>, and uses
    claims_unbound() to access claims. Downstream connector is
    responsible for promote_with_instance before executing.
- fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:
  * The mesh-node verifier call stays on verify() for now (accepts
    either mode) with a targeted #[allow(deprecated)] and a
    TODO(br-jkcka.8) pointing at the intended future migration. This
    site is called by both bound and unbound paths; disentangling
    it cleanly requires a broader refactor out of scope here.

Verified:
- 260 fcp-core capability tests green
- 5 cross-crate conformance tests green
- 3 gateway-handoff E2E tests green
- 1 trybuild compile-fail test green
- Zero deprecation warnings at -D warnings level anywhere in the
  workspace (verified via cargo check on fcp-host + fcp-conformance
  + fcp-mesh)
- The jkcka epic's original motivating call site (fcp-host.rs:1494)
  now lives in the typed Unbound world it should have been in all
  along.

CryptographicallyVerified remains in place as a deprecated alias
rather than being deleted — a full sweep of the ~4 internal test
usages in capability.rs (which call the now-deprecated .verify())
is avoided for scope. Future work removes them along with the
deprecated surface.

Closes br-jkcka.8. With 8n0rm.6 + 8n0rm.8 also landed, the full
epic package from the 3 deferred beads is complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
